title: George Grizzard

[University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill](/wiki/University_of_North_Carolina_at_Chapel_Hill "University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill")

(April 1, 1928 – October 2, 2007) was an American actor.[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1) He was the recipient of a [Tony Award](/wiki/Tony_Award "Tony Award"), a [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"), and a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award "Primetime Emmy Award"), among other accolades.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1928–2007)

| George Grizzard | |
| --- | --- |
| Grizzard and [Patricia Barry](/wiki/Patricia_Barry "Patricia Barry") in the episode "[The Chaser](/wiki/The_Chaser_(The_Twilight_Zone) "The Chaser (The Twilight Zone)")" of the television series *[The Twilight Zone](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone_(1959_TV_series) "The Twilight Zone (1959 TV series)")* | |
| Born | George Cooper Grizzard Jr.  (1928-04-01)April 1, 1928  [Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina](/wiki/Roanoke_Rapids,_North_Carolina "Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina"), U.S. |
| Died | October 2, 2007(2007-10-02) (aged 79)  [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), New York City, U.S. |
| Education | [University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill](/wiki/University_of_North_Carolina_at_Chapel_Hill "University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill") |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1955–2006 |
| Partner | William Tynan |

**George Cooper Grizzard Jr.** (April 1, 1928 – October 2, 2007) was an American actor.[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1) He was the recipient of a [Tony Award](/wiki/Tony_Award "Tony Award"), a [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"), and a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award "Primetime Emmy Award"), among other accolades.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Grizzard&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/Jane_Fonda_George_Grizzard_A_String_of_Beads_1961.jpg/250px-Jane_Fonda_George_Grizzard_A_String_of_Beads_1961.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jane_Fonda_George_Grizzard_A_String_of_Beads_1961.jpg)

Grizzard and [Jane Fonda](/wiki/Jane_Fonda "Jane Fonda") in a 1961 presentation of [W. Somerset Maugham](/wiki/W._Somerset_Maugham "W. Somerset Maugham")'s *A String of Beads*

### Early years and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Grizzard&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early years and education")]

Grizzard was born in [Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina](/wiki/Roanoke_Rapids,_North_Carolina "Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina") and raised in Washington, DC. He once told an interviewer that he was "an only child and probably very lonely, so I made up children to play with — Gene and Bounds and Mrs. Pig and Mrs. Hog and their children and a town called Scottina. It was all a child's fantasy, but I guess that just kind of developed into wanting to create people."[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1) He appeared in student productions in junior high school and decided to become an actor while attending [Woodrow Wilson High School](/wiki/Jackson-Reed_High_School "Jackson-Reed High School") in Washington, where he was president of the drama club. He went on to the [University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill](/wiki/University_of_North_Carolina_at_Chapel_Hill "University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill"), where he studied advertising and drama.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

### Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Grizzard&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Career")]

He returned to Washington after graduation to work in advertising while appearing in [amateur productions](/wiki/Amateur_theatre "Amateur theatre"). He began his professional acting career in 1950 at Washington's [Arena Stage](/wiki/Arena_Stage "Arena Stage"), appearing in some of its earliest productions, with his first leading role in *[Dark of the Moon](/wiki/Dark_of_the_Moon_(play) "Dark of the Moon (play)")*. He went to New York frequently for stage roles, and studied with [Sanford Meisner](/wiki/Sanford_Meisner "Sanford Meisner"), [Philip Burton](/wiki/Philip_Burton_(theatre_director) "Philip Burton (theatre director)"), and [Alan Schneider](/wiki/Alan_Schneider "Alan Schneider").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

Grizzard's stage debut was as the Miner in *[The Corn Is Green](/wiki/The_Corn_Is_Green "The Corn Is Green")* in 1944.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) He made his [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") debut in *[The Desperate Hours](/wiki/The_Desperate_Hours_(play) "The Desperate Hours (play)")* in 1955, about jailbreakers terrorizing a family, in which he played Hank Griffith, the younger brother of a character played by [Paul Newman](/wiki/Paul_Newman "Paul Newman").[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1) He frequently appeared in the plays of [Edward Albee](/wiki/Edward_Albee "Edward Albee"), and was in the original 1962 production of *[Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?](/wiki/Who%27s_Afraid_of_Virginia_Woolf%3F "Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?")* He played Nick alongside [Melinda Dillon](/wiki/Melinda_Dillon "Melinda Dillon") as his wife Honey, a young couple visiting [Uta Hagen](/wiki/Uta_Hagen "Uta Hagen") and [Arthur Hill](/wiki/Arthur_Hill_(Canadian_actor) "Arthur Hill (Canadian actor)") as the warring spouses, Martha and George.[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1) The role won him a [Grammy Award for Best Spoken Word Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Spoken_Word_Album "Grammy Award for Best Spoken Word Album") along with his castmates.

Grizzard played an unscrupulous [United States Senator](/wiki/United_States_Senator "United States Senator") in the film *[Advise and Consent](/wiki/Advise_and_Consent_(film) "Advise and Consent (film)")* in 1962. Beginning in 1963, Grizzard was a member of the original company of the [Guthrie Theater](/wiki/Guthrie_Theater "Guthrie Theater") in Minneapolis, which also included [Jessica Tandy](/wiki/Jessica_Tandy "Jessica Tandy") and [Hume Cronyn](/wiki/Hume_Cronyn "Hume Cronyn"). He played the title role in the Guthrie's inaugural production of *[Hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet "Hamlet")* alongside Tandy, and featured in many productions from the Guthrie's first two seasons, including roles in *[Henry V](/wiki/Henry_V_(play) "Henry V (play)")*, *[Three Sisters](/wiki/Three_Sisters_(play) "Three Sisters (play)")*, and *[Saint Joan](/wiki/Saint_Joan_(play) "Saint Joan (play)")*.

Grizzard also appeared on Broadway in *The Disenchanted* in 1958, *Face of a Hero* in 1960 and *[Big Fish, Little Fish](/wiki/Big_Fish,_Little_Fish "Big Fish, Little Fish")* in 1961.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

His film roles included the drama *[From the Terrace](/wiki/From_the_Terrace "From the Terrace")* with [Paul Newman](/wiki/Paul_Newman "Paul Newman") (1960), the [Western](/wiki/Western_film "Western film") *[Comes a Horseman](/wiki/Comes_a_Horseman "Comes a Horseman")* with [Jane Fonda](/wiki/Jane_Fonda "Jane Fonda") (1978), and a Neil Simon comedy, *[Seems Like Old Times](/wiki/Seems_Like_Old_Times_(film) "Seems Like Old Times (film)")* (1980).[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1)

He also appeared in the 1996 revival of *[A Delicate Balance](/wiki/A_Delicate_Balance_(play) "A Delicate Balance (play)")* and the 2005 revival of *[Seascape](/wiki/Seascape_(play) "Seascape (play)")*. He also starred in *[You Know I Can't Hear You When the Water's Running](/wiki/You_Know_I_Can%27t_Hear_You_When_the_Water%27s_Running "You Know I Can't Hear You When the Water's Running")*. He won the 1996 Tony Award for [Best Actor in a Play](/wiki/Tony_Award_for_Best_Performance_by_a_Leading_Actor_in_a_Play "Tony Award for Best Performance by a Leading Actor in a Play") for *A Delicate Balance*. Additional Broadway credits include *[The Creation of the World and Other Business](/wiki/The_Creation_of_the_World_and_Other_Business "The Creation of the World and Other Business")*, *[The Glass Menagerie](/wiki/The_Glass_Menagerie "The Glass Menagerie")*, *[The Country Girl](/wiki/The_Country_Girl_(1950_play) "The Country Girl (1950 play)")*, *[The Royal Family](/wiki/The_Royal_Family_(play) "The Royal Family (play)")*, and *[California Suite](/wiki/California_Suite "California Suite")*.[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1)

His many television credits include guest-starring roles on shows such as *[The Twilight Zone](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone_(1959_TV_series) "The Twilight Zone (1959 TV series)")*, *[Alfred Hitchcock Presents](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock_Presents "Alfred Hitchcock Presents")*, *[Ironside](/wiki/Ironside_(1967_TV_series) "Ironside (1967 TV series)")*, *[Rawhide](/wiki/Rawhide_(TV_series) "Rawhide (TV series)")*, *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*, *[Ben](/wiki/Ben "Ben")*, *[Dr. Kildare](/wiki/Dr._Kildare_(TV_series) "Dr. Kildare (TV series)")*, *[The Golden Girls](/wiki/The_Golden_Girls "The Golden Girls")*, *[The Cosby Show](/wiki/The_Cosby_Show "The Cosby Show")*, and *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Grizzard guest-starred several times during the 1990s on the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") television drama *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* as defense attorney Arthur Gold. He also portrayed President [John Adams](/wiki/John_Adams "John Adams") in the Emmy Award winning [PBS](/wiki/PBS "PBS") [miniseries](/wiki/Miniseries "Miniseries") *[The Adams Chronicles](/wiki/The_Adams_Chronicles "The Adams Chronicles")*, produced by [WNET](/wiki/WNET "WNET"). In 1980, he won an Emmy for his work in *[The Oldest Living Graduate](/wiki/The_Oldest_Living_Graduate "The Oldest Living Graduate")*. He starred as reporter Richard Larsen in *[The Deliberate Stranger](/wiki/The_Deliberate_Stranger "The Deliberate Stranger")*, a television movie about serial killer [Ted Bundy](/wiki/Ted_Bundy "Ted Bundy").[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1)[[4]](#cite_note-lat-obit-4)

Grizzard declined to discuss his acting technique, saying it was intensely personal, and that he "didn't think it was anybody's business." But he once said that actors needed to "have this mystery to lure an audience in order for them to do part of your work, to involve them. Don't do it all for them."[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

He was inducted into the [American Theater Hall of Fame](/wiki/American_Theater_Hall_of_Fame "American Theater Hall of Fame") in 2002.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Grizzard&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Death")]

Grizzard died in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") of complications from [lung cancer](/wiki/Lung_cancer "Lung cancer") in 2007. According to his *[New York Times](/wiki/New_York_Times "New York Times")* obituary, his only survivor was his partner, William Tynan.[[1]](#cite_note-nyt-obit-1)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_Grizzard&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Filmography")]
